name: Shapiro v. Thompson
summary: Right to travel is fundamental right; one-year residency requirement for welfare benefits impermissibly burdens right to travel and denies equal protection.
holding: Durational residency requirements for welfare benefits unconstitutional; right to travel interstate is fundamental right protected by equal protection and privileges and immunities; requirement lacks compelling state interest.
constitutional_basis: Fourteenth Amendment Equal Protection, Privileges and Immunities Clause
relevance_to_audit: Establishes fundamental right to interstate travel; relevant to restrictions on movement, residency requirements, and identity control measures.
